design_philosophy.core_loop: Assign limited skills to individual lemmings in real-time to guide them safely from entrance to exit; pause to plan, execute during active gameplay. Player must save a specified number or percentage to advance.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited skill uses per level) vs. time pressure (level timer); spatial puzzle-solving vs. real-time execution constraint.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Indirect control of a group; shepherding small creatures through dangerous obstacles using problem-solving rather than reflexes. Empowerment through creative tool use.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Lemmings is opinionated about puzzle design: it rejects direct character control in favor of environmental manipulation via assigned behaviors. It enforces real-time skill assignment despite pause ability—planning must happen mid-execution. It values lateral thinking: multiple valid solutions exist for most levels. The design refuses to hand-hold; failure is frequent and expected as part of learning.

difficulty.continues_system: Level-based progression; failure restarts the current level. No traditional lives or continues.
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-gated progression. Game organized into four difficulty categories (Fun, Tricky, Taxing, Mayhem). Player advances to next category only after completing sufficient levels in current category.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High. Player controls lemming release rate (adjustable from initial to 99 per minute). Pause function allows inspection and planning, but real-time skill assignment remains mandatory. Skill assignment is irreversible; mistakes cost resources. Player chooses which lemmings to sacrifice.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty-gated: four rating tiers unlock sequentially (Fun → Tricky → Taxing → Mayhem → Sunsoft Special). Player must complete/rescue sufficient lemmings in each category to unlock next. Two-player mode available. Password system allows level skipping.
replayability_hooks: Percentage-based completion thresholds encourage replaying to achieve higher rescue rates
Multiple valid solutions per level reward experimentation
Time-based ratings (speed on rescues) provide optimization challenge
Two-player competitive mode with shared level design but separate success conditions

world_building_techniques: Thematic level naming reflects puzzle logic (Tricky level 'Lovely Jubilee' vs. Japanese 'Luvly Jubly')
Environmental hazards suggest surreal/dark fantasy world (lava, traps, explosives)
Terrain tileset theming (dirt, rock, steel barriers) creates visual progression and implicit difficulty escalation

danger_signals: Time limit mechanic creates urgency; all lemmings explode if timer expires
Environmental hazards (lava, traps, fire, excessive heights) serve as obvious fail-states
Lemming death is frequent, visible, and quantified in scoring—normalizes small-scale sacrifice
environmental_storytelling: Hazard placement telegraphs danger zones; lemmings naturally walk toward obstacles
Level architecture encodes puzzle logic: bridges must be built, walls must be tunneled
Terrain variation (soft dirt vs. hard steel) subtly communicates what skills will work
SNES version censored religious imagery (crosses → signs) and replaced dark references ('666', 'death' → 'terminate') to meet Nintendo content policies
